_meta.purpose: Per-host MCP-config mechanism table -- the single source of truth BOTH the `wire` and `unwire` ops (task-015) key off. This is DATA, not per-host code: adding or correcting a host is a single edit to the `hosts` object below, inherited by both ops without touching any script.
_meta.source: task-013 findings.md §5 "Committed data artifact rows" (verified per-host mechanism table, web-verified 2026-07-08)
_meta.feature: feature-004-connection-wiring (Layers & Components: "the wiring twin exposes a symmetric pair of ops that both read the same per-host mechanism table ... DATA, not per-host code")
_meta.hold_no_secret_value: Every entry_template `env` value below is an interpolation reference (${VAR} -- name only), never a secret value. No exceptions.

confidence_meaning.CONFIRMED: Safe to wire now: the mechanism is verified against a repo artifact or official host docs, and the host is installed today.
confidence_meaning.verify-at-install: The mechanism is documented (official docs / best-evidenced candidate) but the host is not installed today; re-verify this row against the actual installed host before the first real write to it. wire-only-installed (feature-004 SPEC) means these rows are carried for coverage and skipped cleanly until their host enters `.aid/settings.yml` `tools.installed`.

notes: KI-007 (out-of-repo writes): codex, copilot-cli, and antigravity default to user-home config paths, outside the repo tree. Any real write there needs its own idempotency and an installed-host check before touching home config.

KI-006 (codex TOML write): Python's stdlib `tomllib` is read-only (no writer), so the codex write path cannot be a pure read-merge-write the way the JSON hosts can -- it needs a line-oriented `[mcp_servers.<name>]` section edit or an added TOML-writer dependency. Carried as verify-at-install.

Filename traps: copilot-cli uses the hyphenated `mcp-config.json` (not `mcp.json`); antigravity uses `mcp_config.json` (underscore). Do not normalize either to the bare `mcp.json` the other hosts use.
Remote-key trap: antigravity uses `serverUrl` (not `url`) for remote (non-stdio) servers -- matters only if a connector ever emits a remote entry.

env interpolation: `${VAR}` passthrough is the intended form for every host below, but support must be confirmed per installed host; where unsupported, the committed-safe fallback is to omit `env` entirely so the server inherits the ambient environment. No host config ever stores a secret value -- only the variable name.

cursor.config_file_note: project; a global `~/.cursor/mcp.json` is also supported but the project file wins on conflict and is AID's committed target

codex.config_file_note: CODEX_HOME default is ~/.codex; a project-scoped .codex/config.toml exists for trusted projects only and is not the default
codex.scope_note: NOT committed -- project scope is opt-in/trusted, not the default
codex.servers_container_note: each server is its own `[mcp_servers.<name>]` table, not a single JSON-style object/array
codex.entry_template_note: env may be written inline (env = { <VAR> = "${<VAR>}" }) or as its own [mcp_servers.<name>.env] sub-table; ${VAR} interpolation support in TOML is verify-at-install -- if codex does not resolve it, the committed-safe fallback is to omit env entirely

copilot-cli.config_file_note: COPILOT_HOME overrides the base directory. Filename is hyphenated mcp-config.json, not mcp.json.
copilot-cli.scope_note: user-home is the documented default write target for `copilot mcp add`; a project-scoped .copilot/mcp-config.json and an auto-discovered .github/mcp.json are also supported but are not the default -- confirm which the installed CLI actually writes to before wiring

antigravity.config_file_note: two live candidates across sources; ~/.gemini/config/mcp_config.json is preferred (newer, shared across IDE+CLI) with ~/.gemini/antigravity/mcp_config.json as a documented fallback -- probe the installed host to confirm the actual path before the first write
antigravity.scope_note: no documented project scope -- user-home only
antigravity.entry_template_note: remote (non-stdio) servers use serverUrl (not url) plus headers -- only relevant if a connector emits a remote entry
